records.ety: [See Counterpoint, corrupted into counterpane, from the employment of pane-shaped figures in these coverlets. ]

defs: A coverlet for a bed, -- originally stitched or woven in squares or figures.
quotes: On which a tissue counterpane was cast.

records.ety: [OF. contrepan a pledge, security; contre + pan a skirt, also, a pawn or gage, F. pan a skirt. See Pane, and cf. Pawn.]

defs: A duplicate part or copy of an indenture, deed, etc., corresponding with the original; -- now called counterpart.
quotes: Read, scribe; give me the counterpane.
